insight:
Yesterday was a solid recovery day with some encouraging signs! Your 6,987 steps kept you lightly active without any formal training, and your body responded beautifully. That 44 bpm resting heart rate shows excellent cardiovascular fitness, while your low stress score of 30/100 indicates you're managing pre-race nerves well. Most importantly, you burned 2,399 calories, putting you right on target.

Your HRV of 118ms is sitting above your baseline range of 93-116ms, which is fantastic news this close to race day! This elevated reading, combined with your low stress levels, suggests your body has recovered well and is primed for performance. You can confidently stick to your planned session today - no modifications needed.

Focus on sleep quality tonight since yesterday's 6.7 hours was slightly short for optimal recovery. Consider an earlier bedtime and limit screen time after 9 PM to help your body continue this excellent recovery trajectory heading into race weekend.
